diagnosis: The playbook command explicitly passes --count 5000 with a default rate of 100 pkt/s. At ~100 pkt/s actual throughput, sending 5000 frames takes ~50s which exceeds the 30s timeout. The fix has two parts: (1) reduce the playbook count to 2000 and add --rate 500, so at 500 pkt/s the attack completes in ~4s; (2) raise the default --rate in src/attacks/mac_flooding.py from 100 to 500 and update the registry default count to 2000 and expose the rate parameter.

files_changed.description: Changed mac-flooding example command from --count 5000 to --count 2000 --rate 500 so the attack completes in ~4s at 500 pkt/s, well within the 30s timeout
Increased default --rate from 100 to 500 frames per second so the attack runs faster by default
Updated mac-flooding registry entry: count default raised to 2000, rate param added with default 500

notes: The root problem is that the playbook hardcodes --count 5000 which at 100 pkt/s takes ~50s. By both raising the rate to 500 pkt/s and reducing the count to 2000, the attack now takes approximately 4s (2000 / 500 = 4s) to complete. No Docker rebuild is needed since all files are pure Python loaded at runtime. The rate parameter is now also exposed in the registry so it can be set via the mag CLI.
